exemplar.title:
How do different concentrations of Vitamin C from Citrus reticulata (mandarin), Citrus
paradisi (grapefruit), Citrus limon (lemon), and a standardized Vitamin C solution (from
tablets) influence the prevention of apple browning over a period of 1 hour, as measured
by light absorption at specific wavelengths using a colorimeter?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How do different concentrations of Vitamin C from Citrus reticulata (mandarin), Citrus
paradisi (grapefruit), Citrus limon (lemon), and a standardized Vitamin C solution (from
tablets) influence the prevention of apple browning over a period of 1 hour, as measured
by light absorption at specific wavelengths using a colorimeter?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question situated in a highly specific food-chemistry context with clear mechanistic justification
Methodological considerations are explained (choice of wavelength, replicates, control of variables) beyond mere listing
weaknesses: Quantification of juice vitamin C concentrations and filter specifications are missing
Some procedural details (blender speed, timeâpoint sampling) remain ambiguous
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands show strong contextualization (band 5) and well-explained considerations (band 5), but the methodology description has ambiguities (band 4). On balance a mid-band mark of 3/6 reflects high quality yet acknowledges reproducibility gaps.

strengths: Data tables include means and standard deviations for multiple trials
Processing (SD calculation) is relevant and correctly applied
weaknesses: Graph axes and raw control data are missing, reducing precision of communication
Uncertainties beyond standard deviation (instrument, propagation) are not considered
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of processing is clear enough but lacks consistent precision (band 3). Uncertainty treatment is limited (band 2). An overall mark of 2/6 balances these moderate strengths with significant omissions.

strengths: Conclusion directly addresses which treatment showed greatest inhibition and references mean values
weaknesses: No statistical validation weakens consistency with analysis
Comparisons to scientific context remain largely qualitative without depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusion is relevant (band 3) but not fully consistent or justified by analysis (lacks stats, band 3) and context comparison is shallow (band 3). Given the single descriptive link and absence of quantitative support, a mark of 1/6 is appropriate.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified (juice variability, PPO levels, temperature)
Improvements proposed directly address identified limitations
weaknesses: Impact of each limitation is not quantitatively analyzed
Explanations of how improvements alter data quality are brief
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation names specific limitations and realistic improvements (band 3), but stops short of explaining their relative impact (band 3). A score of 2/6 reflects solid description while noting the lack of deeper explanation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion summarises that the tablet solution showed greatest inhibition while mandarin was least effective, directly referencing the mean absorbance values. Because no inferential statistics were run, the linkage is descriptive rather than fully justified, so it meets the requirement for being relevant but not fully consistent with analysis, placing it in band 3-4; mark 3 chosen.

A data table with five trials per treatment, means and standard deviations is presented. Although headings are cluttered, the trials and processed values are understandable. Raw absorbance data for the control are referenced but not shown, and axis units/labels for graphs are absent. Thus communication is clear enough but not consistently precise, aligning with band 3-4; mark 3 is assigned.

Means and standard deviations have been calculated correctly; the processing is relevant to the question. However, there is no statistical test (e.g., t-test or ANOVA) to judge significance, and one control mean mentioned in the text (1.516) is absent from the table, indicating minor inconsistency. Processing is therefore appropriate but with notable omissions, fitting band 3-4; mark 3 awarded.

Specific weaknessesâvariable vitamin C content in juices, uncontrolled PPO levels, ambient temperatureâare named and briefly linked to possible data influence, but relative impact is not analysed quantitatively. This corresponds to describing specific limitations (band 3-4); mark 3 awarded.

The student relates findings to antioxidant theory, citric acid pH effects and literature on vitamin Câs role, providing some comparison to accepted scientific context. Depth is limited and largely qualitative, but it surpasses a superficial mention, aligning with band 3-4; mark 3 given.

The research question is clearly focused on vitamin C concentration from four defined sources and its effect on enzymatic browning of apples; extensive background on PPO activity, o-quinone formation and antioxidant action situates the question within a very specific food-chemistry context. Citations and mechanistic equations demonstrate the appropriateness of the context, satisfying the descriptor for a specific and appropriate context (band 5-6).

Uncertainty treatment is limited to calculating standard deviation for repeat trials. Instrumental error of the colorimeter, pipette tolerance, and propagation are not addressed. This demonstrates limited but existent consideration, matching the 1-2 band; the upper mark (2) is given because SD is correctly applied.

Improvements such as standardising vitamin C concentration, extending observation time and using spectrophotometry are clearly linked to the earlier-stated weaknesses, albeit without detailed explanation of how each change alters data quality. Hence they are described rather than fully explained, matching band 3-4; score 3.

The student discusses choice of wavelength (420 nm), need for filtration of pulp, use of replicates, control of apple variety, slice size and exposure time. Potential confounding factors such as PPO variability and unknown vitamin C content in juices are acknowledged in the Evaluation. These points show an explanation rather than a mere list of methodological considerations; hence band 5-6 is appropriate, though some aspects (e.g., temperature control) remain thin, so the lower mark of 5 is awarded.

Step-by-step procedure lists masses, volumes, filtration and measurement details that would allow another student to repeat the work. Nevertheless, concentrations of vitamin C in each juice are not quantified, and time-point sampling is limited to a single 60-min reading; these omissions introduce ambiguity. Therefore the description is reproducible with a few ambiguities, fitting the 3-4 band; the stronger end (4) is chosen because most operational details are present.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly and precisely stated, specifying fruit sources, measurement method, and timeframe, situating the investigation in a well-defined context.
The question could be strengthened by indicating the expected range of vitamin C concentrations in each juice, clarifying the need to quantify their actual concentrations in the methodology.
The aim restates the research question effectively but would benefit from being accompanied by a hypothesis predicting relative inhibition strengths based on known vitamin C levels.
The student provides a comprehensive discussion of PPO activity in apples, effectively grounding the experiment in relevant biochemical context.
The mechanistic description is detailed, but the student could enhance clarity by linking each reaction step explicitly to the subsequent colorimetric measurement of o-quinones.
The inclusion of enzyme reaction equations using LaTeX format is precise and supports reproducibility of the biochemical justification.
Consider adding intermediate reaction kinetics or reaction rates to explain how vitamin C competition influences o-quinone formation over time.
The role of laccases is noted, but quantifying their relative activity versus catechol oxidases would strengthen methodological considerations for enzyme variability.
The description of ascorbic acidâs reduction of o-quinones is clear and accurately frames the experimental rationale.
Independent variable description omits the control condition; explicitly naming the water-only treatment would improve methodological clarity and reproducibility.
The dependent variable is defined qualitatively; consider specifying the quantitative parameter (absorbance change at 420 nm) here for precision.
Controlled variables are detailed comprehensively, demonstrating thorough methodological planning to minimize variability.
The description of filtration needs details on pore size or filter grade to ensure reproducible removal of particulates affecting absorbance readings.
The concentration ranges for colorimetric detection are cited, but the student should explain how the apple solution fits within these ranges or how dilutions will be applied.
The proportionality principle is correctly stated; including an equation (LambertâBeer law) here would improve the clarity of data processing expectations.
The washing step prevents contamination, but indicating water temperature or sanitizer use would strengthen reproducibility and safety considerations.
Procedure for blending is clear, but specifying blender speed and blending duration would eliminate ambiguity in sample preparation.
Including photographs enhances methodological transparency; consider adding scale bars or annotations indicating sample identity.
The juice extraction method is described, but the yield (volume per fruit) and repeatability should be quantified to assess vitamin C variability.
Adjusting to 50 mL ensures consistency, but the student should measure final concentration of vitamin C in each juice to validate the standard solution assumption.
The incubation period is justified by preliminary trials; consider sampling at multiple time points to model browning kinetics rather than a single 60 min endpoint.
Mention of numbered solutes is useful; ensure that blank corrections and baseline calibrations are described for accurate absorbance data processing.
Selection of 420 nm is appropriate for o-quinone detection; explaining how potential overlapping absorbance by other compounds was assessed would strengthen uncertainty considerations.
Measuring at additional wavelengths shows good precaution; however, the student should report these readings or explain why they were not included in the final analysis.
The sample placement description is clear, but the student should specify the path length and cuvette geometry used in the colorimeter to ensure reproducibility.
Data table provides means and standard deviations adequately; including control data in the table would improve clarity of comparative analysis.
The use of the sample standard deviation formula is appropriate; a brief note on why nâ1 is used here (unbiased estimator) could deepen methodological rigor.
The analysis interprets mean intensities effectively, but it lacks statistical significance testing to support claims of differences between treatments.
Attributing high intensity to other constituents is plausible; consider measuring pH or sugar content to substantiate this hypothesis quantitatively.
The evaluation acknowledges controlled variables and random errors, demonstrating awareness of reliability factors but could quantify instrument error and propagate uncertainties.
Proposed improvements are realistic and relevant; linking each suggestion explicitly to specific limitations would deepen the evaluation.
The conclusion restates key findings and aligns with the research question, but it overemphasizes color intensity without statistical validation, limiting its consistency with the analysis.
Identifying experimental strengths shows reflective insight; consider differentiating between instrument precision and biological variability for clarity.
The extension ideas broaden the investigation scope well; specifying how variable concentrations would be controlled and measured would enhance feasibility.
The bibliography includes relevant sources, but inconsistencies in citation formats should be corrected to adhere to a single style guide.

strands.name: Research question context

strands.name: Data processing quality

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Improvements
